tipRules.tip: Johnston: Use a drop-shot with a 3/8-ounce weight and a 4-inch worm on a No. 2 Gamakatsu G-Finesse hook for deepwater smallmouth on Lake Ontario in 18 to 42 feet over rock structure.
Johnston: Use Power-Pole Drift Paddles to slow your drift in big water with 6-foot waves. Drift with the wind using LiveScope to cover a lot of water while drop-shotting.

knowledge.insight: Johnston: Run a milk run on Lake Ontario starting at the closest spot and working out to the farthest, then come in. Use Garmin LiveScope to cover water while drifting. Pretty simple - just cover a lot of water over rock structure in 18 to 42 feet.
Johnston: Local knowledge is invaluable. Having a lot of spots on Lake Ontario means you only need two days of pre-fishing when you know the place well.
Johnston: On big water, commit to the lake when the wind direction gives you shelter from a point or land feature. Staying sheltered can reduce 8-foot waves to 6-footers, which is fishable.
